6d9d0db0-48f4-411b-aab1-618b609dc017.name: 丹田气息基础 (Dantian Breath Foundation)
6d9d0db0-48f4-411b-aab1-618b609dc017.description: Establish dantian (丹田) abdominal breath support — the core energy center for all Peking Opera vocalization
6d9d0db0-48f4-411b-aab1-618b609dc017.instructions:
1. Stand in the classic opera stance: feet shoulder-width apart, knees very slightly bent
2. Place both hands on your lower abdomen (丹田 — two fingers below the navel)
3. Inhale slowly through the nose — feel the lower abdomen expand outward like a balloon
4. Exhale on a slow 'si' (嘶) sound, maintaining the expansion as long as possible
5. Practice sustaining the abdominal engagement for 15–20 seconds per breath
6. Repeat 5 times — this 丹田 support is the foundation of 气息 (breath power) in Jingju
7. All Peking Opera vocal power flows from this center — without it, the voice has no base

7c981263-a0dc-4c13-abb7-55275262b400.name: 咬字归韵 (Consonant Attack & Vowel Resolution)
7c981263-a0dc-4c13-abb7-55275262b400.description: Master the two-stage articulation of Peking Opera: clear consonant attack (咬字) followed by resonant vowel extension (归韵)
7c981263-a0dc-4c13-abb7-55275262b400.instructions:
1. Peking Opera vowels come in final-categories (韵辙): speak each slowly: 'a', 'o', 'e', 'i', 'u', 'ü'
2. For each syllable: attack the consonant crisply, then melt into a sustained vowel
3. Practice on classic 韵辙 rhymes: 言前辙 (yan-qian) — 'tian', 'mian', 'lian'
4. Sing each syllable on a single note: sharp 't-' then long 'iiian' extension
5. The consonant should feel like a tiny flick of the tongue — not heavy
6. The vowel should resonate with 行腔 — a sustained, melodically active tone
7. This 咬字归韵 technique is what makes Peking Opera text perfectly intelligible even in large theaters

e7c956a8-4618-4128-a718-1b5d4de8ab3b.name: 旦角嗓音入门 (Dan Role Voice Introduction)
e7c956a8-4618-4128-a718-1b5d4de8ab3b.description: Discover the distinctive 旦角 (female role) voice — a stylized, forward-placed falsetto that blends chest and head resonance
e7c956a8-4618-4128-a718-1b5d4de8ab3b.instructions:
1. The 旦角 voice is NOT a natural female voice — it is a highly stylized, elevated, head-resonant tone
2. Start by humming on 'n' — feel vibration high in the nasal cavity and forehead
3. Transition to 'nii' — a bright, narrow, forward sound; keep the larynx medium-high
4. Sing '啊' on a comfortable higher pitch with this forward, bright placement
5. The 旦角 tone should feel 'placed at the eyebrow' — not in the throat
6. Practice '一二三四五' on this bright placement
7. Beginners: if you feel throat strain, you are pushing too hard — lighten the tone and think forward

0bfa2203-d955-4923-933c-542309dbe6b5.name: 生角嗓音入门 (Sheng Role Voice Introduction)
0bfa2203-d955-4923-933c-542309dbe6b5.description: Explore the 生角 (male hero role) voice — a resonant, dignified, chest-dominant tone with controlled head voice mixing
0bfa2203-d955-4923-933c-542309dbe6b5.instructions:
1. The 生角 (especially 老生, mature male) voice is mid-chest dominant with a dark, authoritative ring
2. Begin by speaking in a slightly deeper, more resonant voice than normal — feel the chest vibrate
3. Sing '啊' in chest voice on a comfortable mid-range note — rich, not hollow
4. Now try '嗯' to find a nasal-chest blend — this is the 老生 mix
5. The 生角 tone should feel 'grounded in the chest but lifted in the face'
6. Practice on the word '我' (wǒ) — attack with strength, sustain with resonance
7. 小生 (young male): uses a higher, more lyrical tone — lighter than 老生, brighter than 旦角

d1852c85-0577-4b3c-b312-320555521bf3.name: 韵白吟诵 (Stylized Recitative)
d1852c85-0577-4b3c-b312-320555521bf3.description: Learn 韵白 — the melodic, heightened speech-song that Peking Opera actors use for dramatic declamation between sung passages
d1852c85-0577-4b3c-b312-320555521bf3.instructions:
1. 韵白 is sung-speech: it has rhythm and melody but follows the natural tones of Mandarin
2. Choose a classic 韵白 phrase, e.g.: '将身儿来至在大街口'
3. First, speak it with exaggerated Mandarin tones — feel each 声调 as a melodic gesture
4. Now sing it: let the 4 tones become melodic rises and falls
5. Add a slight vibrato on sustained syllables, especially at phrase ends
6. The rhythm of 韵白 is free — not strict, like operatic recitative
7. 韵白 practice trains your ear for 声调配合 (tone-melody alignment) — the core challenge of Peking Opera

e0a46200-7b89-4ceb-b250-e0b16048d0a0.name: 五声音阶发声 (Pentatonic Scale Vocalization)
e0a46200-7b89-4ceb-b250-e0b16048d0a0.description: Build your voice on the pentatonic scale (五声调式) — the fundamental tonal system of all Chinese opera music
e0a46200-7b89-4ceb-b250-e0b16048d0a0.instructions:
1. The pentatonic scale: do-re-mi-sol-la (宫-商-角-徵-羽) — there is no 'fa' or 'si'
2. Sing the ascending pentatonic scale on '啊': 1-2-3-5-6
3. Hold each note for 2 counts — even tone, good breath support
4. Now descend: 6-5-3-2-1
5. Move up a half step and repeat — cover several keys
6. Notice: the pentatonic scale has a distinctly different sound from Western major/minor scales
7. In Peking Opera, melodies never use fa or si — if you land on these notes, you are off-pitch for the style

ff36755c-3b94-49ea-a5fd-e0cd3eb77072.name: 拖腔技法入门 (Melismatic Extension Introduction)
ff36755c-3b94-49ea-a5fd-e0cd3eb77072.description: Begin developing 拖腔 — the art of extending a single syllable through an elaborate melodic phrase, one of Peking Opera's most expressive techniques
ff36755c-3b94-49ea-a5fd-e0cd3eb77072.instructions:
1. 拖腔 means 'dragging the phrase' — one syllable becomes a flowing melodic journey
2. Start simple: sing '啊' and let the pitch move freely up, down, and around while staying on one breath
3. Add subtle ornamentation: a small upward turn, a brief grace note, a gentle wobble
4. Practice with the syllable '呀' — a common 拖腔 syllable in 旦角 arias
5. Now try a known melody phrase and extend the final syllable into a 4–8 note 拖腔
6. Keep the breath steady throughout — 丹田 support holds the phrase together
7. 拖腔 should feel organic, not mechanical — it expresses the emotion the character cannot say directly

4a595dd0-b2c9-47b7-bb59-87f02a458aa4.name: 念字节奏训练 (Text Rhythm Training)
4a595dd0-b2c9-47b7-bb59-87f02a458aa4.description: Develop the rhythmic clarity of 念字 — the precise, rhythmically charged text delivery that drives Peking Opera drama
4a595dd0-b2c9-47b7-bb59-87f02a458aa4.instructions:
1. Peking Opera uses strict rhythmic patterns (板眼): 一板一眼, 一板三眼, 散板
2. Clap a simple 一板一眼 pattern (2/4 time): STRONG-weak, STRONG-weak
3. Speak a text phrase in time with the clap — every syllable lands precisely on a beat or half-beat
4. Common 念字 drill: '东-南-西-北' — clap on each syllable
5. Now add a small pitch contour to each syllable following its Mandarin tone
6. Accelerate the clapping gradually — keep the text precise at all speeds
7. In Peking Opera, a single mistimed syllable throws off the entire ensemble — precision is a discipline

46561bd9-86fc-4bd6-9367-5adefca00321.name: 二黄慢板行腔 (Erhuang Manban Phrasing)
46561bd9-86fc-4bd6-9367-5adefca00321.description: Navigate the slow, majestic 二黄慢板 — the most expressive slow tempo section in Peking Opera, requiring long breath and sustained 行腔
46561bd9-86fc-4bd6-9367-5adefca00321.instructions:
1. 二黄 (Erhuang) is a major melodic system of Peking Opera — associated with 老生 and serious 旦角 arias
2. 二黄慢板 is the slowest, most ornate form — one phrase can span 4–8 bars
3. Practice sustaining a single phrase on '啊' for 20+ seconds with 行腔 (melodic movement)
4. The melody should rise and fall like a wave — never static, never rushing
5. Key rule: in 二黄, the dominant note centers around 'sol' and 'mi' of the pentatonic
6. Add 韵味 (flavor): small ornamental turns at cadence points are essential — straight tone sounds empty
7. Record yourself and listen — does the phrase breathe and move naturally, or does it sound mechanical?

efa55eaa-1d27-4f9f-8aa7-28773a0b67ea.name: 西皮唱腔训练 (Xipi Melodic Training)
efa55eaa-1d27-4f9f-8aa7-28773a0b67ea.description: Master 西皮 — the brighter, more energetic melodic system of Peking Opera, contrasting with the deeper 二黄
efa55eaa-1d27-4f9f-8aa7-28773a0b67ea.instructions:
1. 西皮 (Xipi) is higher and more lively than 二黄 — it is used for heroic, joyful, or agitated moods
2. The dominant note in 西皮 sits a perfect fifth higher than in 二黄
3. Practice a 西皮 riff on '啊': start high, descend in a flowing pentatonic pattern, cadence low
4. 西皮 phrase endings often have a characteristic upward or downward mordent — add these ornaments
5. Practice switching from 二黄 to 西皮 on the same vowel — feel the tonal shift
6. 西皮 requires more agility than 二黄 — the melodic leaps are larger and more frequent
7. At full speed, 西皮快板 can be extremely fast — build the foundation slowly before accelerating

9c086778-9771-4794-9115-9d4d458b0bbf.name: 花腔装饰技法 (Hua Qiang Ornamentation)
9c086778-9771-4794-9115-9d4d458b0bbf.description: Develop 花腔 ornamentation — the cascading melodic decorations that embellish Peking Opera phrases with drama and virtuosity
9c086778-9771-4794-9115-9d4d458b0bbf.instructions:
1. 花腔 (huā qiāng) means 'flowery vocal pattern' — ornamental runs over one or more syllables
2. Begin with a simple single-note phrase, then add a 4-note turn around the main pitch
3. Practice the classic '甩腔' (swing phrase): descend from a high note, swing around the middle, land low
4. Add 倚音 (grace notes): a quick note just above or below the main pitch before landing
5. Practice slowly at first — clarity of each ornament is more important than speed
6. The ornaments should follow the natural 声调 of the word being sung
7. Common mistake: rushing through ornaments — each note in a 花腔 run must be distinct and intentional

20f9f55b-e758-4955-9b4f-fc27917c02e7.name: 旦角假声精进 (Dan Role Falsetto Refinement)
20f9f55b-e758-4955-9b4f-fc27917c02e7.description: Refine the stylized 旦角 falsetto — developing a smooth, resonant, controlled head voice that can sustain dramatic phrases
20f9f55b-e758-4955-9b4f-fc27917c02e7.instructions:
1. The 旦角 voice sits in a stylized high register — neither natural speech nor Western soprano
2. Practice the 旦角 pitch slide: from a mid-chest note, glide upward into the head register smoothly
3. Hold a sustained high '啊' in the 旦角 register for 10+ seconds — no cracks, no wobble
4. Add controlled vibrato on the sustained tone — 旦角 vibrato is narrower than Western opera (≈30 cents)
5. Now practice descending: from the head register, move through the passaggio gracefully without a 'break'
6. The 旦角 换声区 (register transition) is critical — singers must train this daily
7. Advanced: sustain a high note while adding 行腔 movement — dynamic melodic activity on the same breath

42969358-a82d-4d48-ac20-a474dac1398d.name: 净角铜锤唱法 (Jing Role Power Voice)
42969358-a82d-4d48-ac20-a474dac1398d.description: Explore the 净角 (painted face) voice — a large, resonant, chest-dominant sound with strong projection and dramatic weight
42969358-a82d-4d48-ac20-a474dac1398d.instructions:
1. 净角 roles use a large, forceful voice with strong chest resonance — think 'mountain and thunder'
2. Stand in a wide, grounded stance — the 净角 voice is rooted in the body
3. Sing '啊' with maximum chest resonance — feel the vibration all the way down to the sternum
4. 净角 tone is big but not shouty — power comes from the 丹田, not from throat constriction
5. Practice long sustained notes at forte volume — the 净角 must project over full orchestra
6. Add a characteristic 净角 vibrato: slightly wider and slower than 旦角 (≈50 cents, 5–6 Hz)
7. 净角 singing is one of the most physically demanding in all Chinese opera — build stamina gradually

67bd6745-67c6-429c-ab41-b828adb9144c.name: 快板节奏与咬字 (Kuaiban Articulation Speed)
67bd6745-67c6-429c-ab41-b828adb9144c.description: Develop the precision and speed needed for 快板 — the rapid-tempo section where both articulation and pitch must remain perfectly clear
67bd6745-67c6-429c-ab41-b828adb9144c.instructions:
1. 快板 (kuài bǎn) means 'fast tempo' — in 西皮快板, tempo can exceed 200 BPM
2. Start slow: sing the text '一更里头来打坐' on a simple pentatonic melody, one syllable per beat
3. Each syllable must have clear consonant attack (咬字) and a brief vowel resonance
4. Gradually increase the tempo by 10 BPM per repetition
5. At fast speeds: the consonant attack must become more precise, not less
6. Common fault: vowels blur at speed — combat this with exaggerated consonant articulation in practice
7. Goal: reach 140+ BPM with every syllable perfectly articulate — this is the standard for professional 快板

c14d31f9-98d1-4709-8c0c-711c7766960f.name: 戏剧内心独白 (Dramatic Inner Monologue)
c14d31f9-98d1-4709-8c0c-711c7766960f.description: Connect vocal technique to dramatic character — every phrase must be driven by the character's inner emotional state
c14d31f9-98d1-4709-8c0c-711c7766960f.instructions:
1. Choose a character situation: a hero facing death, a woman awaiting her lover, a general in battle
2. Read the aria text as a dramatic monologue — what does each line MEAN to the character?
3. Identify the emotional peak of the aria — where is the character most overwhelmed?
4. Sing the aria with this subtext firmly in mind — every melodic phrase should express the character's emotion
5. In Peking Opera, expression and technique are inseparable — hollow technique is immediately visible
6. Practice with a mirror — your face and body posture must match the character's state
7. Rule: in Jingju, the audience watches the eyes (眼神) as much as they listen to the voice

a5042213-49a9-4c0b-b8b3-a9e59ac37eef.name: 声调配合训练 (Tone-Melody Alignment)
a5042213-49a9-4c0b-b8b3-a9e59ac37eef.description: Train the critical skill of aligning Mandarin tonal language with Peking Opera melody — the unique challenge that defines Chinese operatic composition
a5042213-49a9-4c0b-b8b3-a9e59ac37eef.instructions:
1. Mandarin has 4 tones: 1st (flat high), 2nd (rising), 3rd (dip), 4th (falling)
2. In Peking Opera, the melody must generally follow these tones — going against them creates misinterpretation
3. Practice: say '妈麻马骂' — the 4 tones as a musical shape
4. Now set these 4 tones to a pentatonic melody where the pitch contour mirrors the tone direction
5. Find a phrase from a real aria: identify where tones are 'correct' vs. where the composer inverted them for drama
6. Inversions are allowed for dramatic emphasis — but the singer must compensate with clear articulation
7. This training transforms your brain to think melodically AND linguistically simultaneously — a rare cognitive skill

9179f165-a73b-4a9a-ac9c-a1c6afb0e61d.name: 完整唱段句演绎 (Full Aria Phrase Performance)
9179f165-a73b-4a9a-ac9c-a1c6afb0e61d.description: Apply all Peking Opera techniques to a complete aria phrase — uniting 丹田 breath, 咬字, 拖腔, 行腔, and dramatic expression
9179f165-a73b-4a9a-ac9c-a1c6afb0e61d.instructions:
1. Select a complete 4–8 bar phrase from a Peking Opera aria you are preparing
2. Warm up with 5 minutes of 丹田 breathing, pentatonic scales, and 咬字 drills
3. Sing the phrase once focusing purely on technique: breath, pitch accuracy, 咬字
4. Sing it a second time with full dramatic and character intent — who are you, what do you feel?
5. Evaluate: was the 行腔 natural? Did the 拖腔 have 韵味? Were the tones intelligible?
6. Identify one specific technical target for the next run-through
7. Record yourself — compare with a master recording of the same aria to understand the gap

6177d801-e920-4147-a1a6-a2d8fe58a6aa.name: 二黄原板完整演唱 (Erhuang Yuanban Full Performance)
6177d801-e920-4147-a1a6-a2d8fe58a6aa.description: Perform a complete 二黄原板 section — the foundational medium-tempo Erhuang form used in the most expressive moments of classic Peking Opera
6177d801-e920-4147-a1a6-a2d8fe58a6aa.instructions:
1. 二黄原板 is medium-slow tempo: serious, contemplative, deeply expressive
2. It requires sustained 行腔 — every phrase must breathe and move melodically
3. Select a famous 二黄原板 excerpt (e.g., from 空城计, 打渔杀家, or 锁麟囊)
4. Sing through the excerpt at half tempo first — ensure every ornament and 咬字 is precise
5. Bring it to performance tempo — maintain the 韵味 (operatic flavor) that makes 二黄 distinctive
6. The emotional quality of 二黄 should feel noble and weighty — never rushed or surface-level
7. A great 二黄原板 should make the audience feel the weight of the character's inner life

d6ace737-dc8f-4936-bd2b-e67b67ece087.name: 西皮快板连贯训练 (Xipi Kuaiban Continuity)
d6ace737-dc8f-4936-bd2b-e67b67ece087.description: Perform an extended 西皮快板 section with unbroken articulation, accurate pitch, and sustained energy
d6ace737-dc8f-4936-bd2b-e67b67ece087.instructions:
1. 西皮快板 is the most demanding tempo form — every syllable must be crisp at high speed
2. Begin at 120 BPM — sing the entire 快板 section with focus on syllable clarity
3. Increase to 150 BPM — notice where your articulation begins to blur; those are your weak points
4. The 丹田 must remain strongly engaged at fast tempos — fatigue leads to vocal strain
5. Breathe at prescribed points only — do not add extra breaths at fast tempos
6. The pitch pattern of 西皮快板 repeats in predictable cycles — internalize the cycle so it becomes automatic
7. Final test: perform at full speed (160–180 BPM for advanced) from start to finish without error

4115dfab-bd34-4894-9f4b-389ba3814508.name: 形体与唱腔融合 (Stylized Gesture + Voice Integration)
4115dfab-bd34-4894-9f4b-389ba3814508.description: Integrate Peking Opera stylized body movement (身段) with vocal performance — the defining unity of Jingju artistry
4115dfab-bd34-4894-9f4b-389ba3814508.instructions:
1. Peking Opera is a total-body art — gesture (手), stance (步), gaze (眼) must all serve the vocal expression
2. Practice standing in the basic 旦角 stance: feet close together, slight weight on front foot, arms graceful
3. As you sing a phrase, add the corresponding hand gesture (水袖 sleeve gesture or hand position)
4. Rule: the gesture should ARRIVE on the melodic peak of the phrase — not before, not after
5. Practice the famous 旦角 head tilt — a slight downward bow on an emotional phrase adds depth
6. Record yourself — does the body match the voice? Or do they feel like two separate performances?
7. A Peking Opera singer who can sing but not move, or move but not sing, is only half an artist

5c894982-4b3e-403b-87de-a408cffcba77.name: 京剧舞台亮相 (Jingju Stage Simulation)
5c894982-4b3e-403b-87de-a408cffcba77.description: Simulate a Peking Opera stage performance — entrance, aria, and exit — with full character commitment and audience awareness
5c894982-4b3e-403b-87de-a408cffcba77.instructions:
1. Set up your space as a stage — mark the entrance, center stage, and exit
2. Enter as your character: the entrance walk followed by 亮相 (striking a pose) announces who you are
3. Hold the opening 亮相 for 2 full seconds before beginning to sing
4. Perform the aria with full technical and dramatic commitment — no stopping, no apologizing
5. During 过门 (instrumental interludes), maintain character expression — do NOT go blank
6. End with a clear closing pose and exit in character
7. Self-evaluation: did the technique hold under simulated performance pressure? Did the character live?

b83d7845-7f6f-4210-b652-20b9815e135b.name: 二胡伴奏协调 (Erhu Ensemble Coordination)
b83d7845-7f6f-4210-b652-20b9815e135b.description: Train your voice to blend with and follow the 二胡 (erhu) — the primary accompanying instrument of Peking Opera
b83d7845-7f6f-4210-b652-20b9815e135b.instructions:
1. Play a recording of a famous Peking Opera aria with 二胡 accompaniment and sing along
2. The 二胡 is the lead instrument — your voice should follow its pitch and tempo
3. Notice: the 二胡 often plays the melody before you sing — listen and match its tonal quality
4. In Peking Opera, the singer and 二胡 player communicate in real time — learn to listen as you sing
5. The 二胡 uses the inner/outer string system — your pitch must match this precisely
6. If the 二胡 ornaments a phrase, you can match it or contrast it — but always stay rhythmically locked
7. True Peking Opera singing is always a duet between the voice and the 二胡

87f0e3dd-914f-4d8f-8857-a43f741f71e5.name: 角色神韵塑造 (Character Spirit & Presence)
87f0e3dd-914f-4d8f-8857-a43f741f71e5.description: Embody a Peking Opera character completely — developing the 神韵 (spirit-flavor) that separates great performers from technical ones
87f0e3dd-914f-4d8f-8857-a43f741f71e5.instructions:
1. Choose a character: 虞姬 (Consort Yu), 诸葛亮 (Zhuge Liang), 孙悟空 (Monkey King), 包拯 (Judge Bao)
2. Research the character: their story, emotional world, social status, and role type
3. Stand in front of a mirror and 'become' the character before singing a single note
4. The eyes (眼神) are the soul of Peking Opera performance — practice the character's gaze
5. Now sing the aria with this complete character presence — not as yourself, but as the character
6. 神韵 is what makes the audience forget they are watching a performance and feel genuine emotion
7. Final challenge: perform to an actual audience — their reaction tells you if the 神韵 is real

be30f747-9731-444d-9c21-efc042e8ddfe.name: 竞技级综合演绎 (Competition-Level Full Performance)
be30f747-9731-444d-9c21-efc042e8ddfe.description: Deliver a competition-ready Peking Opera performance — all technique, all drama, all character presence at peak level simultaneously
be30f747-9731-444d-9c21-efc042e8ddfe.instructions:
1. Select your strongest aria — one you have worked on throughout Phase 3
2. Perform a complete competition run: entrance, 亮相 (pose), full aria, exit
3. No stopping — treat this as a real competition
4. After completing the performance, objectively evaluate:
   - Pitch accuracy and pentatonic fidelity?
   - 咬字 clarity at all tempos?
   - 行腔 and 拖腔 quality with true 韵味?
   - 神韵 and character presence?
   - Ensemble awareness (did you follow the 二胡 in your mind)?
5. Write down your 3 strongest points and 3 areas for improvement
6. Competition Peking Opera requires perfection in technique AND unique artistic personality
7. The goal is not just to sing well — it is to make the audience remember your performance forever

f7a9e2e5-cb7c-4607-b5ca-7cb6c9ee197e.feedback: Strength: The True Tone Score of 54 indicates a moderate level of timbre quality, which is a positive aspect to build upon.; Strength: The softness detected in the Emotion Detected category could be a valuable expressive tool in certain contexts, such as portraying vulnerability or sensitivity in a character.; Mistake: Inadequate dantian breath support, as indicated by the Breath Score of 0, which is crucial for stable pitch and tone quality.; Mistake: Lack of pitch accuracy and tone stability, reflected in the 0 scores for Pitch Accuracy and Tone Stability Index, suggesting difficulty in establishing a basic pitch reference.; Mistake: Insufficient vocal brightness, with a Tone Brightness score of 19, which may be related to inadequate resonance placement and breathing support.; Suggestion: To improve your Breath Score, place one hand on your lower abdomen (丹田), about two fingers below the navel. As you inhale slowly through your nose, focus on feeling your stomach expand outward like a balloon, pushing your hand away from your spine. Maintain this expansion as you exhale, aiming to sustain it for 15-20 seconds per breath.; Suggestion: Since your Pitch Accuracy and Tone Stability Index scores are 0, let's focus on establishing a basic pitch reference. Hum a gentle 'mmm' sound, feeling the vibration in your mouth and nasal cavities. 8eed3fc9-2c3c-449c-abaa-e0103ba8ea7d.title: 苏三起解
8eed3fc9-2c3c-449c-abaa-e0103ba8ea7d.lyrics:
苏三离了洪洞县，
将身来至大街前。
未曾开言我心内惨，
过往的君子听我言。

哪一位去往南京转，
与我那三郎把信传。
言说苏三把命断，
相逢在阴曹地府间。

324249f0-5455-4a69-9841-306f43debc10.title: 贵妃醉酒·海岛冰轮
324249f0-5455-4a69-9841-306f43debc10.lyrics:
海岛冰轮初转腾，
见玉兔，玉兔又早东升。
那冰轮离海岛，
乾坤分外明。

皓月当空，
恰便似嫦娥离月宫。
高烧银烛，
红霞万朵百花丛中。

89344f70-c048-4e12-80e8-ba31f2dc39fa.title: 四郎探母·坐宫
89344f70-c048-4e12-80e8-ba31f2dc39fa.lyrics:
杨延辉坐宫院自思自叹，
想起了当年事好不惨然。
十五年困居在番邦地面，
受他人冷眼怎不伤心间？

我好比笼中鸟有翅难展，
我好比虎离山受了孤单。
我好比浅水龙困在了沙滩，
我好比波浪中失舵的舟船。

lyricsTimestamps.text: 杨延辉坐宫院自思自叹，
想起了当年事好不惨然。
十五年困居在番邦地面，
受他人冷眼怎不伤心间？
我好比笼中鸟有翅难展，
我好比虎离山受了孤单。
我好比浅水龙困在了沙滩，
我好比波浪中失舵的舟船。

bafe1150-bbfb-48a9-8247-4408b5a8f6a1.title: 空城计·我本是
bafe1150-bbfb-48a9-8247-4408b5a8f6a1.lyrics:
我本是卧龙岗散淡的人，
论阴阳如反掌保定乾坤。
先帝爷下南阳御驾三顾，
算就了汉家业鼎足三分。

官封到武乡侯执掌帅印，
东西征南北剿博古通今。
周文王访姜尚周室大振，
诸葛亮怎比得姜尚半分。

lyricsTimestamps.text: 我本是卧龙岗散淡的人，
论阴阳如反掌保定乾坤。
先帝爷下南阳御驾三顾，
算就了汉家业鼎足三分。
官封到武乡侯执掌帅印，
东西征南北剿博古通今。
周文王访姜尚周室大振，
诸葛亮怎比得姜尚半分。

7580818e-ad6b-45d5-9bc5-930cbd710518.title: 霸王别姬·看大王
7580818e-ad6b-45d5-9bc5-930cbd710518.lyrics:
看大王在帐中和衣睡稳，
我这里出帐外且散愁情。
轻移步走向前荒郊站定，
猛抬头见碧落月色清明。

在哪里——
在哪里——
听四面楚歌声，
羽兮羽兮骓不逝，
虞兮虞兮奈若何？

lyricsTimestamps.text: 看大王在帐中和衣睡稳，
我这里出帐外且散愁情。
轻移步走向前荒郊站定，
猛抬头见碧落月色清明。

62456d45-ab5b-496c-8d61-a62b011e99e4.title: 武家坡·一马离了西凉界
62456d45-ab5b-496c-8d61-a62b011e99e4.lyrics:
一马离了西凉界，
不由人一阵阵泪洒胸怀。
武家坡十八载苦守寒窑，
盼夫君盼得我望眼欲穿。

十八年守空房，
把奴家怎枉配鸳鸯？
公爹爷问寒暖，
只剩得这满腹心肠。

lyricsTimestamps.text: 不由人一阵阵泪洒胸怀。
武家坡十八载苦守寒窑，
盼夫君盼得我望眼欲穿。

9fc6999f-0b46-4f4a-a98e-6bb2e67567cc.title: 打渔杀家·父女们打渔
9fc6999f-0b46-4f4a-a98e-6bb2e67567cc.artist: 打渔杀家 · 余派传统
9fc6999f-0b46-4f4a-a98e-6bb2e67567cc.lyrics:
父女们打渔在芦花荡里，
虽然是贫寒度日子也甜蜜。
自幼儿随父亲水上漂流，
一网下去鱼虾满舱里。

只恨那丁员外横行霸道，
强收租不讲理逼死乡里。
今日里捕得鱼换些酒食，
且顾今日乐不问明日事。

lyricsTimestamps.text: 父女们打渔在芦花荡里，
虽然是贫寒度日子也甜蜜。
自幼儿随父亲水上漂流，
只恨那丁员外横行霸道，
强收租不讲理逼死乡里。
今日里捕得鱼换些酒食，
且顾今日乐不问明日事。

1270f3bd-5a80-4bc9-8c29-edf0838e72ee.title: 锁麟囊·春秋亭外
1270f3bd-5a80-4bc9-8c29-edf0838e72ee.lyrics:
春秋亭外风雨暴，
何处悲声破寂寥？
隔帘只见一花轿，
想必是新婚渡鹊桥。

吉日良辰当欢笑，
为何鲛珠化泪抛？
此时却又明白了，
世上何人不怀抱？

bdd4725f-2bb9-4610-a389-ed5fc2887266.title: 霸王别姬·完整版
bdd4725f-2bb9-4610-a389-ed5fc2887266.lyrics:
看大王在帐中和衣睡稳，
我这里出帐外且散愁情。
轻移步走向前荒郊站定，
猛抬头见碧落月色清明。

在哪里——
在哪里——
听四面楚歌声。

今夜里大王意气衰，
此时间战士已无几。
披戎装到帐外把兵巡视，
一阵风吹动了银白发丝。

虞姬啊，虞姬，虞姬——
奈何！奈何！
奈若何？

lyricsTimestamps.text: 看大王在帐中和衣睡稳，
我这里出帐外且散愁情。
轻移步走向前荒郊站定，
猛抬头见碧落月色清明。
披戎装到帐外把兵巡视，
一阵风吹动了银白发丝。

5e019bb9-7803-4309-be92-b33d58261c5e.title: 梨花颂
5e019bb9-7803-4309-be92-b33d58261c5e.lyrics:
梨花开，春带雨，
梨花落，春入泥。
此生只为一人去，
道是无情却有情。

天下再难觅良辰，
一点一滴梨花泪。
梨花开，梨花谢，
梨花飞，梨花飞。

隔世已难相思寄，
两茫茫，此情悲。

ee05d3b2-ca95-4257-8ed1-8e36e6ecad02.title: 红灯记·都有一颗红亮的心
ee05d3b2-ca95-4257-8ed1-8e36e6ecad02.lyrics:
都有一颗红亮的心，
学做革命的新人。
我家的表叔数不清，
没有大事不登门。

虽说是，虽说是，
亲眷们打断骨头连着筋，
可他比亲眷还要亲。

爹爹和奶奶，
革命的情意深。
他们的奋斗牺牲，
都为着革命把根寻。

lyricsTimestamps.text: 亲眷们打断骨头连着筋，

052202b0-5b15-487f-9e63-561d02592a0a.title: 穆桂英挂帅·我不挂帅
052202b0-5b15-487f-9e63-561d02592a0a.lyrics:
我不挂帅谁挂帅？
我不领兵谁领兵？
叫声穆桂英——
休得犹豫！

你本是杨家将之后，
保定江山护社稷。
披挂重上战场去，
杀退番邦保太平。

穆桂英，穆桂英，
为国捐躯情愿意！
